meta.title: A Cloning Strategy for the Bacteriophage T4 vs Gene

abstractHtml: The product of the bacteriophage T4 vs gene, the τ peptide, has been shown to thermally stabilize temperature-sensitive valyl-tRNA synthetase (EC 6.1.1.9[EC] ). To clone the bacteriophage T4 vs gene, recombinant pBR322-T4 DNA molecules were used to transform Escherichia coli CP 790302 (valSts). Transformants that grew at 43.5 °C on selective medium exhibited some properties indicative of a phage-modified valyl-tRNA synthetase. The data are consistent with successful cloning of the vs gene and atypical modification of the valyl-tRNA synthetase in CP 790302.
